pullRequest.title: feat(pr): add /pr skill to create a PR then babysit ×3

nodes.body:
_🎯 Functional Correctness_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Detect the repository’s actual default branch, partner.**

This only protects `main` and `master`; repositories using names such as `develop` or `trunk` can still open a PR from their default branch. Resolve the default branch from `gh repo view` or the remote HEAD before allowing creation.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md` around lines 49 - 52, Update the stop
conditions in the PR workflow documentation to detect the repository’s actual
default branch via `gh repo view` or the remote HEAD, rather than checking only
`main` and `master`; prevent PR creation when currently on that branch without a
feature branch, while preserving the existing dirty-working-tree checks.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:triton:luna -->

<!-- cr-indicator-types:potential_issue -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

Addressed in https://github.com/paulbaranowski/wild-horses/commit/3d5f3de773385cf1f58f30cc94edd9928e8230f0. Preflight now resolves the repo default branch via `gh repo view` (with remote HEAD fallback) instead of hard-coding main/master. 🤖 cb-babysit:addressed v1 core@3.16.1

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
git fetch origin 3d5f3de773385cf1f58f30cc94edd9928e8230f0 --depth=1 2>&1 | tail -5
git show 3d5f3de773385cf1f58f30cc94edd9928e8230f0 -- plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md 2>&1 | head -100
```

Length of output: 5010

---

</details>

`@paulbaranowski` Yep, checked the commit and that's a clean fix, partner. `gh repo view --json defaultBranchRef` now does the resolvin', with the `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD` fallback ridin' shotgun, and the stop condition rightly checks against the repo's actual default branch instead of only `main`/`master`. This rig's ready to roll on `develop`, `trunk`, or whatever branch name the repo's wranglin'. Much obliged!

<!-- <review_comment_addressed> -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->

_🗄️ Data Integrity & Integration_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Check ahead/behind state before deciding whether to push.**

`git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD@{upstream}` only confirms that an upstream exists; it does not reveal whether local commits are ahead. A branch with an existing upstream can therefore create a PR without its latest local commits. Compare `HEAD` with the upstream ref and push whenever the local branch is ahead.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md` around lines 55 - 59, Update the branching
guidance around the upstream check to compare local HEAD with its upstream ref,
not merely confirm that an upstream exists. Use an ahead/behind comparison such
as git status --short --branch or git rev-list to detect local commits, and run
git push -u origin HEAD whenever the branch is ahead or lacks an upstream before
creating the PR.
```

</details>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

Addressed in https://github.com/paulbaranowski/wild-horses/commit/3d5f3de773385cf1f58f30cc94edd9928e8230f0. Push now triggers when there is no upstream or when local HEAD is ahead of upstream (via `git status --short --branch` / rev-list), not merely when an upstream exists. 🤖 cb-babysit:addressed v1 core@3.16.1

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
git log --oneline -5
git show 3d5f3de773385cf1f58f30cc94edd9928e8230f0 -- plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md | head -100
```

Length of output: 3659

---

</details>

`@paulbaranowski` Yer commit checks out, partner. The updated preflight now pushes whenever the branch has no upstream *or* local `HEAD` is ridin' ahead of it (via `git status --short --branch` / `git rev-list --left-right --count @{upstream}...HEAD`), so unpushed commits won't get left behind at the corral before the PR is opened. That closes the gap I flagged. Much obliged for the quick fix, and I noticed you also tightened up the `gh pr create` invocation to be shell-safe while you were in there — good trail-riding.

<!-- <review_comment_addressed> -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->

_🎯 Functional Correctness_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Pass the title and arguments through shell-safe variables, partner.**

Substituting the generated title into `--title "<title>"` can misparse titles containing quotes, backslashes, or command-substitution characters. Unquoted `$ARGUMENTS` also permits word splitting and glob expansion. Store validated arguments in an array and use `--body-file -` with the heredoc instead.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md` around lines 73 - 84, Update the PR creation
command to pass the generated title through a validated, shell-safe variable and
invoke it as a quoted argument; parse validated arguments into an array to
prevent word splitting and glob expansion, converting bare branch names to a
base-branch flag. Replace command-substituted body handling with a single-quoted
heredoc piped via `--body-file -`, while preserving PR URL capture after `gh pr
create` in the documented workflow.
```

</details>

Howdy, `@paulbaranowski` — I rode on over and checked out commit 3d5f3de773385cf1f58f30cc94edd9928e8230f0, and yessir, that's a clean fix. The `TITLE` variable's quoted proper, `CREATE_ARGS` array keeps them flags from gettin' word-split or glob-happy, and swappin' the command-sub body for `--body-file -` with that single-quoted heredoc keeps the body's `$`, backticks, and quotes ridin' straight and true. That'll hold up just fine, partner.

<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->

### Skip-create path undefined **Medium Severity** Preflight offers option (a) to skip `gh pr create` and only babysit an existing PR, but the skill never defines what happens after the user picks (a). Phases 2–3 still assume no PR exists, so an agent may run `pr-summary-writer` and `gh pr create` anyway, conflicting with the duplicate-PR rule and Phase 4’s “PR you just opened” wording.
Additional Locations (1) - [`plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md#L66-L71`](https://github.com/paulbaranowski/wild-horses/blob/3d5f3de773385cf1f58f30cc94edd9928e8230f0/plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md#L66-L71)
 
Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit 3d5f3de773385cf1f58f30cc94edd9928e8230f0. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).

Addressed in https://github.com/paulbaranowski/wild-horses/commit/be5654ed10aaa7ff4411d2b464a83e27312d07f6. Preflight option (a) now skips Phases 2–3 and jumps to Phase 4 with the existing PR URL; Phase 4 wording updated accordingly. 🤖 cb-babysit:addressed v1 core@3.16.1

### Apostrophe breaks title assignment **Medium Severity** Phase 3 stores the summary-writer title via `TITLE='…'` single-quoted assignment. Titles containing a single quote (common in conventional messages) terminate the string early and break the shell command before `gh pr create`, despite the skill’s goal of shell-safe create invocation.
 
Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit 3d5f3de773385cf1f58f30cc94edd9928e8230f0. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).

Addressed in https://github.com/paulbaranowski/wild-horses/commit/be5654ed10aaa7ff4411d2b464a83e27312d07f6. Title is now assigned via a single-quoted heredoc into `TITLE`, so apostrophes no longer terminate the string. 🤖 cb-babysit:addressed v1 core@3.16.1

### Rules omit ahead-of-upstream push **Medium Severity** The **Rules** section says to push only when the branch is not on the remote, but Phase 1 also requires a push when an upstream exists and local `HEAD` is ahead. An agent leaning on the summary rule can skip that push and open a PR without the latest local commits.
Additional Locations (1) - [`plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md#L60-L61`](https://github.com/paulbaranowski/wild-horses/blob/be5654ed10aaa7ff4411d2b464a83e27312d07f6/plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md#L60-L61)
 
Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit be5654ed10aaa7ff4411d2b464a83e27312d07f6. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).

Addressed in https://github.com/paulbaranowski/wild-horses/commit/2a9f25f28408686e4e021204d20415d8a9befc22. Rules now require a push when there is no upstream or when local HEAD is ahead of upstream, matching Phase 1. 🤖 cb-babysit:addressed v1 core@3.16.1

### Babysit-only path skips push **Medium Severity** Preflight option (a) sends the flow straight to Phase 4, but push is only motivated as needed before `gh pr create`, and the Rules restate push as “before create.” On babysit-only runs, unpushed local commits can be left off the remote while `cb-babysit` tends a PR that still points at an older SHA.
Additional Locations (1) - [`plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md#L138-L139`](https://github.com/paulbaranowski/wild-horses/blob/2a9f25f28408686e4e021204d20415d8a9befc22/plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md#L138-L139)
 
Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit 2a9f25f28408686e4e021204d20415d8a9befc22. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).

Addressed in https://github.com/paulbaranowski/wild-horses/commit/50e1908b2dd6f7e7b171c544450a3faa8ad035a5. Option (a) now runs the push step when needed before jumping to Phase 4; Rules updated to match. 🤖 cb-babysit:addressed v1 core@3.16.1

📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough The PR adds a Claude and Cursor `pr` plugin, registers its manifests, and defines a `/pr` skill that validates the current branch, creates a pull request with generated content, then runs up to three CI and review babysitting passes. ### Changes **Pull request workflow** |Layer / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Plugin registration and manifests**
`.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`, `.cursor-plugin/marketplace.json`, `plugins/pr/.claude-plugin/plugin.json`, `plugins/pr/.cursor-plugin/plugin.json`|Registers the `pr` plugin and adds matching version `0.1.0` manifests with author and workflow metadata.| |**Skill contract and preflight**
`plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md`|Defines `/pr` arguments and dependencies, reads composed skills, validates repository and branch state, prevents duplicate PRs, and pushes the branch when needed.| |**PR creation and babysitting**
`plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md`|Generates the PR title and body, creates the PR with `gh pr create`, captures its URL, and runs up to three `cb-babysit` passes with defined stop conditions.| **Estimated code review effort:** 3 (Moderate) | ~20 minutes ### Sequence Diagram(s) ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant GitRepository participant pr-summary-writer participant gh participant cb-babysit GitRepository->>GitRepository: Validate branch, upstream, working tree, and existing PR pr-summary-writer->>gh: Supply generated title and body gh->>GitRepository: Create pull request GitRepository-->>gh: Return pull request URL cb-babysit->>gh: Run CI and review feedback pass gh-->>cb-babysit: Return pass outcome ```
